title: daucus carota subsp. sativus
intro: Daucus carota subsp. sativus, commonly known as the cultivated carrot, is a subspecies of the wild carrot plant that has been selectively bred for its edible root. This plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, as well as its ability to support digestion and boost the immune system. The health benefits of Daucus carota subsp. sativus include reducing the risk of certain cancers, improving eye health, and supporting bone health. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be made from the root, leaves, and seeds of the plant.
